diagrams.title: State Transition Diagram for Concurrent Activities
diagrams.description: A formal state machine diagram showing three states (inactive, active, completed) with event-driven transitions. Arrows indicate possible state changes with labeled events.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how activities transition between states based on events in the Concurrent Model

diagrams.title: Timeline Comparison: Sequential vs Concurrent Execution
diagrams.description: Gantt chart comparing sequential vs concurrent execution for activities with dependencies
elements: Bar chart Row 1 (Sequential): Activity A (days 1-5), Activity B (days 6-12), Activity C (days 13-18)
Bar chart Row 2 (Concurrent): A and B start together (days 1-5 for A, 1-7 for B), C waits for B then runs (days 12-17)
Vertical dashed lines showing synchronization points
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how concurrency reduces project duration through parallel execution

diagrams.title: Synchronization Mechanisms in Concurrent Development
diagrams.description: Three scenarios illustrating different synchronization types used to coordinate concurrent activities
elements: Panel 1: Join Synchronization - Activity B waits at barrier until Activity A completes
Panel 2: Barrier Synchronization - Multiple activities wait at milestone before proceeding
Panel 3: Communication Synchronization - Arrow showing data exchange between parallel activities
diagrams.purpose: Explain how synchronization manages dependencies in concurrent development
